refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve and maintain operational availability of ≥ 0.97 (97%) over the customer-defined evaluation period, measured as (total scheduled operating time − unplanned downtime) / total scheduled operating time.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-004 is a key performance indicator (KPI) that directly impacts mission success and stakeholder confidence. Refinement clarifies the measurement basis (scheduled vs. unplanned downtime), establishes the quantitative target (≥0.97), and defines the evaluation window. This is critical for safety-critical heavy equipment where availability directly correlates with operational safety and productivity.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Continuous telemetry logging of operational state transitions (ready, operating, degraded, failed); automated calculation of availability ratio; periodic audit against maintenance records and incident logs; customer acceptance sign-off at end of evaluation period.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: The NGHE solution shall detect and log all unplanned downtime events (failures, faults, safety stops) with timestamp and root cause classification.
FR-NGHE-004.2: The NGHE solution shall implement preventive maintenance scheduling to minimize unplanned downtime.
FR-NGHE-004.3: The NGHE solution shall provide rapid fault diagnosis and recovery mechanisms to restore operational readiness.
FR-NGHE-004.4: The NGHE solution shall maintain real-time availability status visibility to Site Manager and Maintenance teams.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement redundant critical subsystems (dual motors, dual control channels, dual safety circuits) with automatic failover to eliminate single points of failure.
DP-B: Implement comprehensive health monitoring, predictive diagnostics, and condition-based maintenance scheduling to detect degradation before failure.
DP-C: Implement rapid fault isolation and modular component replacement strategy with pre-positioned spare parts and trained maintenance teams.
DP-D: Implement integrated combination of redundancy (DP-A), health monitoring (DP-B), and rapid recovery (DP-C) with coordinated fault management.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Implement integrated availability architecture combining: (1) redundant critical subsystems with automatic failover for safety-critical functions; (2) comprehensive health monitoring with predictive diagnostics and condition-based maintenance; (3) rapid fault isolation, modular replacement, and pre-positioned spares strategy; (4) coordinated fault management logic that prioritizes safety over availability.

potential_sources: Redundancy logic must not compromise safety (failover must not mask safety-critical faults).
Health monitoring must not introduce latency in safety-critical control loops.
Maintenance scheduling must respect operational safety constraints and worksite policies.
coupling_check.notes: DP-D is decoupled from specific vehicle architecture, teleoperation mode, or safety service implementation. It defines a logical availability strategy that can be realized across multiple physical and software implementations. Coupling risk is manageable through clear fault management prioritization rules.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Customer evaluation period is ≥ 30 days of continuous operation to provide statistically meaningful availability measurement.
Assumption: Spare parts supply chain can support rapid replacement (lead time < 4 hours for critical components).
Risk: Redundancy adds cost, weight, and complexity; may conflict with cost or performance targets.
Risk: Predictive diagnostics accuracy depends on historical failure data; new equipment may lack sufficient training data.
Risk: Rapid recovery depends on trained maintenance personnel availability on-site; remote sites may have longer recovery times.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-D should be refined to explicitly define: (1) which subsystems are redundant vs. monitored vs. rapidly replaceable (allocation matrix); (2) fault management decision logic (safety priority rules); (3) maintenance response time SLAs by fault severity; (4) availability measurement methodology and data collection requirements.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: System-level availability orchestration module responsible for integrating redundancy, health monitoring, fault management, and maintenance coordination across all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). Owns the availability measurement, reporting, and coordinated fault response logic.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives health status, operational state, and fault signals from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules. Used for availability calculation and predictive diagnostics.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operational tasks from Site Manager; may pause/resume/modify tasking based on availability state and maintenance scheduling.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Transmits safety-critical fault notifications and failover events to Safety Services and Site Manager.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Transmits availability metrics, maintenance schedules, and diagnostic data to remote monitoring systems; receives remote maintenance directives.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural system-level owner of availability orchestration. Availability is a system-level KPI that requires coordination across all subsystems. Minimal diffusion: only requires interfaces to subordinate modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) which are not yet decomposed.

Vehicle module owns hardware redundancy and fault detection for propulsion/control, but availability KPI is system-level. Assigning to Vehicle would fragment availability logic across multiple modules and create coupling with Teleoperation and Safety Services.

Teleoperation owns remote control availability, but not overall system availability. Assigning here would miss Vehicle and Safety Services contributions to availability and create inappropriate coupling.

Safety Services owns safety-critical fault response, but not overall availability measurement or maintenance scheduling. Assigning here would conflate safety with availability and create inappropriate dependencies.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Availability is a system-level KPI that requires coordinated measurement, fault management, and maintenance scheduling across all subsystems. NGHE Solution is the appropriate system-level owner. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services will own their respective contributions to availability (redundancy, health monitoring, rapid recovery) but will report status via Telemetry interface to NGHE Solution for orchestration.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution module may become a coordination bottleneck if fault management logic is overly centralized. Mitigation: Define clear decision rules and delegate local fault response to Vehicle/Teleoperation/Safety Services where possible.

Risk: Availability measurement depends on accurate Telemetry from all subsystems. If Telemetry interface is incomplete or unreliable, availability calculation will be invalid. Mitigation: Define Telemetry data requirements and validation rules explicitly.
Risk: Maintenance scheduling may conflict with operational tasking. Mitigation: Define clear prioritization rules (safety > availability > productivity) and escalation procedures.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: NGHE Solution IM should be further refined to define: (1) availability measurement algorithm and data schema; (2) fault management decision tree (which faults trigger failover vs. maintenance vs. safety stop); (3) maintenance scheduling constraints and SLAs; (4) interfaces to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services for health status reporting and coordinated response.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-D (integrated availability architecture) directly addresses FR-NGHE-004 (≥0.97 availability). The combination of redundancy, health monitoring, and rapid recovery is logically sound and well-established in safety-critical systems. DP-D is not over-constrained and allows flexibility in physical/software realization.

diffusion_risks: Redundancy logic (DP-A component) will be distributed across Vehicle module (hardware redundancy) and Safety Services module (safety failover). NGHE Solution will own the orchestration logic but not the implementation details. This is acceptable if interfaces are clearly defined.

Health monitoring (DP-B component) will be distributed across Vehicle (propulsion/control health), Teleoperation (communication health), and Safety Services (safety system health). NGHE Solution will aggregate via Telemetry interface. Acceptable if Telemetry schema is comprehensive.

Rapid recovery (DP-C component) will be owned by Maintenance/Quality stakeholder and supported by NGHE Solution's maintenance scheduling and diagnostic reporting. Acceptable if maintenance SLAs are defined.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: DP-D is appropriately contained in NGHE Solution as the system-level orchestrator. Subordinate modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) will own specific availability mechanisms (redundancy, monitoring, recovery) but will report to NGHE Solution via Telemetry and SafetyAlert interfaces. This is a clean hierarchical decomposition.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Availability measurement is feasible via continuous Telemetry logging and automated calculation. Verification requires: (1) definition of Telemetry data schema and collection requirements; (2) availability calculation algorithm; (3) evaluation period duration and acceptance criteria; (4) audit procedures for validation.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: FR-NGHE-004 is allocated to NGHE Solution as the system-level owner. Sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-004.1 through 004.4) will be allocated to subordinate modules in next decomposition level. Allocation is clear and traceable.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-D to include explicit fault management decision logic (which faults trigger failover, maintenance, or safety stop).
Define Telemetry data schema and collection requirements to support availability measurement.
Define maintenance response time SLAs by fault severity and location.
Establish availability measurement methodology and acceptance criteria with customer before evaluation period begins.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-004 is a system-level KPI that requires decomposition into sub-FRs addressing specific availability mechanisms (fault detection, preventive maintenance, rapid recovery, status visibility). DP-D is sound but requires further refinement through sub-FR decomposition. NGHE Solution is the correct IM owner, but subordinate modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) must be decomposed to allocate specific availability responsibilities. Proceeding to next level is justified and necessary.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: The NGHE solution shall detect and log all unplanned downtime events (failures, faults, safety stops) with timestamp and root cause classification.
FR-NGHE-004.2: The NGHE solution shall implement preventive maintenance scheduling to minimize unplanned downtime.
FR-NGHE-004.3: The NGHE solution shall provide rapid fault diagnosis and recovery mechanisms to restore operational readiness.
FR-NGHE-004.4: The NGHE solution shall maintain real-time availability status visibility to Site Manager and Maintenance teams.

assumptions: Customer evaluation period is ≥ 30 days of continuous operation.
Spare parts supply chain can support rapid replacement (lead time < 4 hours).
Trained maintenance personnel are available on-site or within 4-hour response time.
Telemetry infrastructure can reliably collect health status from all subsystems.
Safety-critical faults take priority over availability optimization.
open_risks: Redundancy adds cost and complexity; may conflict with cost targets.
Predictive diagnostics accuracy depends on historical failure data.
Maintenance response time depends on on-site personnel availability.
Availability measurement depends on accurate Telemetry; incomplete data will invalidate results.
Fault management logic must balance safety, availability, and productivity; conflicting priorities may arise.
evidence_needed: Evaluation period duration and measurement methodology.
Spare parts availability and maintenance response time SLAs.
Historical failure data for predictive diagnostics training.
Worksite safety plan and operational policies that constrain maintenance scheduling.
Telemetry data schema and collection requirements.
Fault management decision logic and prioritization rules.
